title: Adenia stricta
taxonomy: Adenia stricta is classified within the family Passifloraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the passionflower family. This family consists of around 1,000 species of plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Passifloraceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Adenia stricta belonging to the tribe Passifloreae. Adenia stricta itself is a species of flowering plant native to tropical Africa, and its classification is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies. The exact classification of Adenia stricta is Adenia stricta (Benth.) Engl., which reflects its classification within the Passifloraceae family.
uses: Adenia stricta is a plant species used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. intro: Adenia stricta, commonly known as the climbing cactus or elephant creeper, is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae. It has been traditionally used in various parts of the world, including Africa and Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and inflammatory conditions, as well as exerting antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Adenia stricta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Adenia stricta is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a paste or ointment, to treat various health conditions.
